proposals.reason: All 5 failures share a single root cause: API test data uses '@test.local' as email domain. Pydantic email-validator rejects '.local' as RFC2606 reserved TLD, returning HTTP 422 from the SUT before any business logic runs. Fix is purely test-side: replace email domain literals from '<name>@test.local' to '<name>@example.com' (also RFC2606-reserved but accepted by email-validator). No assertion expected values change. No product code touched.

patch_plan.description: Replace every email literal containing '@test.local' with '@example.com' (preserve username portion). This affects approximately 12 occurrences across test functions.
constraints: must not change any assertion's expected status code or message
must not delete any test function
must not change role_ids, password, or username values

patch_plan.description: Update the non_admin_user_factory default email computation: change f'{username}@test.local' to f'{username}@example.com'.
constraints: must not change factory return shape

patch_plan.description: If any helper has a hardcoded '@test.local' default email, replace with '@example.com'.

verification.expected_effect: All 5 API tests progress past the 422 validation error and either pass or surface different (deeper) failures.

proposals.reason: All 6 E2E tests fail in the SAME setup line: tests/e2e/conftest.py:65 calls expect(page).not_to_have_url(lambda url: '/login' in url). Playwright's expect(page).not_to_have_url() does NOT accept a lambda — it requires a string or compiled regex. The previous wait_for_url(lambda) succeeded; the login is functionally complete. The redundant lambda-based expect crashes the fixture before any test body runs. Fix: remove the redundant expect-with-lambda assertion (wait_for_url already enforced the same condition).

patch_plan.description: Remove line: expect(page).not_to_have_url(lambda url: '/login' in url). The preceding page.wait_for_url(lambda url: '/login' not in url, timeout=ADMIN_LOGIN_TIMEOUT_MS) already enforces this condition. No assertion is weakened — wait_for_url throws TimeoutError if URL never changes, which is identical guarantees.
constraints: must not delete the wait_for_url call

patch_plan.description: Replace every '@test.local' literal with '@example.com'. Same RFC2606 reserved-TLD root cause as FIX-001 — Pydantic email-validator rejects '.local' but accepts 'example.com'. Without this, every E2E test that creates a user via UI form will hit the backend create endpoint with an invalid email and surface a 422 error toast.
constraints: must not change any expect(...) assertion's expected value
must not change unique_username, password, or role names
must not delete any test function

verification.expected_effect: All 6 E2E tests progress past fixture setup; then pass or surface real downstream issues.

proposals.reason: Test sends 'ghost@test.local' as a payload field (not just for filtering). Pydantic UserUpdate.email is EmailStr → 422 validates BEFORE the controller can return 404 for the nonexistent user_id. To exercise the 404 path, the email field must pass schema validation. Replace 'ghost@test.local' with 'ghost@example.com'. The 404 expected-value assertion is unchanged — this is a test_data_failure, not an assertion_failure (the 422 is upstream of the assertion under test).

patch_plan.description: In test_update_user_not_found, change 'ghost@test.local' to 'ghost@example.com' inside the request payload.
constraints: must not change the assertion: response.status_code == 404

proposals.reason: Inspect classified these as assertion_failure but the actual root cause is identical to FIX-001/FIX-003 — '@test.local' email literal causes 422 BEFORE the assertion is reached. The recorded assertion mismatch (422 == 200/404) is a SYMPTOM of the data validation error, not a genuine product behavior assertion. The fix is purely test_data: replace '@test.local' → '@example.com'. No expected_value change; no product code touched. Reclassifying from assertion_failure to test_data_failure based on log evidence (RequestValidationError on email field).

patch_plan.description: In test_delete_user_success: change 'qa_delete_user@test.local' to 'qa_delete_user@example.com'. In test_reset_password_normal_user_success: change 'qa_reset_user@test.local' to 'qa_reset_user@example.com'.
constraints: must not change reset_password expected payload (123456)
must not change the 200/404 status assertions
must not change usernames or password fields

verification.expected_effect: Both tests pass; delete returns 200, reset returns 200, and password 123456 authorizes the new login.

not_eligible.reason: SUT product behavior: AuthControl.is_authed uses Header(...) (required field). When the token header is missing, FastAPI returns 422 RequestValidationError (Pydantic-level) before the auth dependency runs. The case TC-USER-003 specified expected = 401, but the actual SUT contract is 422 for missing-header, 401 only for invalid/expired token. This is a genuine spec-vs-impl mismatch; auto-fixing the test would require changing the assertion expected value, which is forbidden. Recommended: file a product-bug ticket OR update case spec to match SUT contract (separate review required).

The aws-inspect classifier categorized 4 API failures (test_list_users_unauthenticated_rejected, test_update_user_not_found, test_delete_user_success, test_reset_password_normal_user_success) as assertion_failure based on the 'assert X == Y' pattern in stack traces. Manual log review reveals 3 of the 4 (all except unauthenticated_rejected) are actually test_data_failure (RequestValidationError on email field). FIX-003 and FIX-004 reclassify 3 of these as eligible test_data_failure based on log evidence; only test_list_users_unauthenticated_rejected is truly assertion-vs-product-spec. Documenting this drift for inspect classifier improvement.
